title: Success stories of the club development program of the Department of Cultural Affairs and Sport
description: OUDTSHOORN NEWS - Two junior players who are...
bodyText:
[{"type":"paragraph","text":"OUDTSHOORN NEWS - Two junior players who are affiliated to the Union Stars Club in Oudtshoorn recently excelled at the Cricket South Africa (CSA) national youth tournaments.","html":"OUDTSHOORN NEWS - Two junior players who are affiliated to the Union Stars Club in Oudtshoorn recently excelled at the Cricket South Africa (CSA) national youth tournaments."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The respective players form part of the Union Stars Women’s team who benefit from the program. The club plays in the Six Gun Grill SWD Women’s Division 1 club competition."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Jezé CampherAt the Cricket South Africa Girls' u/16 National tournament that was hosted in Johannesburg in December the Six Gun Grill Garden Route Badgers won three out of four matches with the last match abandoned due to lightning and rain.","html":"Jezé Campher
At the Cricket South Africa Girls' u/16 National tournament that was hosted in Johannesburg in December the Six Gun Grill Garden Route Badgers won three out of four matches with the last match abandoned due to lightning and rain."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Jeze Campher excelled as she received the award as Most Versatile Player of the tournament and Batter of the Tournament. Jeze Campher is a learner at Morestêr Secondary School in Oudtshoorn and is a member of the Union Stars Women’s team.","html":"Jeze Campher excelled as she received the award as Most Versatile Player of the tournament and Batter of the Tournament. Jeze Campher is a learner at Morestêr Secondary School in Oudtshoorn and is a member of the Union Stars Women’s team."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Emmy-Kay SamsonTwo players from the Six Gun Grill Garden Route Badgers u/19 Girls team, Emmy-Kay Samson (Oudtshoorn School of Skills in Oudtshoorn) and Mariana Breedt (Outeniqua High School in George) were selected for the SA Schools Fillies u/19 team.","html":"Emmy-Kay Samson
Two players from the Six Gun Grill Garden Route Badgers u/19 Girls team, Emmy-Kay Samson (Oudtshoorn School of Skills in Oudtshoorn) and Mariana Breedt (Outeniqua High School in George) were selected for the SA Schools Fillies u/19 team."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The team was selected upon conclusion of the CSA School’s Girl’s u.19 national tournament that was held in Durban in December. Emmy-Kay Samson is a member of the Union Stars Women’s team.","html":"The team was selected upon conclusion of the CSA School’s Girl’s u.19 national tournament that was held in Durban in December. Emmy-Kay Samson is a member of the Union Stars Women’s team."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Ons bring jou die nuutste Tuinroete, Hessequa, Karoo nuus’","html":"Ons bring jou die nuutste Tuinroete, Hessequa, Karoo nuus’"}]

images.alt: Success stories of the club development program of the Department of Cultural Affairs and Sport
